# Transcrição: Adapting_to_different_communication_styles

- **Fonte:** Adapting_to_different_communication_styles
- **Duração:** 01:18
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:18]

There are so many different communication styles in the world and if you just use one communication style to try to communicate with every single other style, you end up not really building strong connections. The fastest way to be able to identify the person's communication style is going to be a little bit different to what you may be thinking. Essentially, while you're listening to somebody speak, you want to listen out for a few things. The first thing is their volume. How loud are they speaking? How quietly are they speaking? The second thing, their pace. How quickly are they speaking? How slowly are they speaking? How are they varying that? Their pitch variants. Do they speak just with one pitch? Or do they change pitches and go all over the place? And then the last one you want to keep track of as well is tonality. Listen for the tonality. Are they feeling the words? Are they being expressive or are they being more reserved? You see, if you just keep track of these four things, now all you have to do is match those four things to the specific person you're communicating with. So again, when you use this technique, it allows you to adapt to absolutely anybody. It's going to push you out of your comfort zone. It's going to make you practice a whole different range of communication styles, which in the end, after years and years of doing this, it will make you a way more flexible communicator. You can adapt to any environment. You can mingle in any crowd and you can meet anybody you need to without scaring them off.
